metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5112
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors generated various antibodies against EFA6D (aka. EFA6R, gene PSD3) isoforms.

Figure 3: biochemical fractions showed EFA6D1b/c was localized to the LP1 fraction where PSD marker protein PSD95 (DLG4) was enriched (but note that these are not highly-stringent PSD fractions) and not presynaptic marker protein synaptophysin (SYP). Conversely, synaptophysin was enriched in LP2 fractions where EFA6D1b/c was absent.

Figure 8: pre-embedding immunoelectron microscopic analysis in the hippocampal CA3 region showed the pan-antibody detected EFA6D in dendritic spines, dendrites and cell body.

"Notably, immunolabeling was hardly detected in the postsynaptic density of dendritic spines by either pre-embedding (Fig. 8A,B,E,F,I,J) or postembedding (data not shown) immunoelectron microscopy with anti-EFA6D1/2a-d, anti-EFA6D1/2s, or anti-panEFA6D1 antibodies.
...
On the other hand, the labeling density of EFA6D1/2s was similar to that of panEFA6D1, with high density in the dendrite, spine, and cell body of CA3 pyramidal cells (Fig. 8H,L).". Experimental description: M&M: "Three male C57BL/6 mice at the 10th postnatal week (CLEA Japan, Tokyo, Japan) were used for immunoperoxidase staining, three for immunofluorescent staining, two for an immunoelectron microscopic analysis, and three for immunoblotting analysis"

Antibody specificity is shown in Figure 2.
